hints: Opposite angles in a cyclic quadrilateral sum to $180°$.
A cyclic quadrilateral has opposite angles summing to 180°.
questions.explanation: Every rectangle has all four angles equal to 90°, so opposite angles trivially sum to 180°. Hence every rectangle is cyclic. Other parallelograms generally aren't.

questions.promptTex: \text{In cyclic quad } ABCD, \, \angle A = 2x, \angle C = x + 30. \\ \text{Find } x.

questions.prompt: To prove four points are concyclic (lie on one circle), it suffices to show:
choices: They form a quadrilateral with one pair of opposite angles supplementary.
They are vertices of an equilateral figure.
They have the same distance from a center.
hints: Converse of the cyclic quadrilateral theorem.
Or option D in a sense — 'same distance from center' IS the definition of being on a circle, but you'd have to find that center.
questions.explanation: By the converse of the cyclic quadrilateral theorem, a quadrilateral with one pair of opposite supplementary angles is cyclic. (Option D is also technically correct but requires finding the center first; option B is the standard angle-chasing tool.)

hints: Let $\angle D = x$. Then $4x + x = 180$, so $x = 36$, $\angle B = 144$.

steps.text: Let $\angle D = x$. Then $4x + x = 180$, so $x = 36$, $\angle B = 144$.

questions.prompt: Is an isosceles trapezoid always cyclic?
choices: Only if the parallel sides are equal
hints: Base angles of an isosceles trapezoid are equal.
The two non-parallel sides being equal forces opposite angle pairs to be supplementary.
questions.explanation: Yes — every isosceles trapezoid is cyclic. The two base angles at each base are equal, and the four angles sum to 360°, which makes opposite angles supplementary.

questions.promptTex: \text{Cyclic quad } ABCD. \, \angle A = 70°, \angle B = 80°. \\ \text{Find } \angle C + \angle D.
questions.explanation: Quadrilateral angles sum to 360°: $\angle C + \angle D = 360 - 70 - 80 = 210°$. (You can also verify: $\angle C = 180 - 70 = 110$, $\angle D = 180 - 80 = 100$, sum $= 210$. ✓)

steps.text: **Solution.** Quadrilateral angles sum to 360°: $\angle C + \angle D = 360 - 70 - 80 = 210°$. (You can also verify: $\angle C = 180 - 70 = 110$, $\angle D = 180 - 80 = 100$, sum $= 210$. ✓)
